questions.question: 下線部 consider の意味を考えるとき、まず見るべき合図はどれ？
questions.why: 下線部の前後から意味を推測する。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: What does the underlined word “consider” mean in the passage?
questions.arrow_answer:
前：each environment has advantages
↓
consider
↓
後：what kind of life suits you best
questions.why: 前後関係から「よく考える」が最も自然。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Which best summarizes the passage?
questions.arrow_answer:
city advantages
↓
countryside advantages
↓
choose what suits you
questions.answer: Both cities and the countryside have good points, so people should choose what suits them.
questions.why: 両方の利点を述べた後、結論で自分に合う生活を選ぶべきと言っている。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Which pair shows the main contrast in the passage?
questions.why: on the other hand が対比の合図。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Why may some people prefer the countryside?
questions.arrow_answer:
fresh air / quiet surroundings
↓
prefer countryside
questions.answer: Because it offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
questions.why: 理由を本文中の具体例から拾う型。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Which sentence supports the idea that cities are convenient?
questions.answer: Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
questions.why: support は根拠文を探す合図。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: What is the writer’s main point?
questions.answer: People should choose the place that suits their own life.
questions.why: 最後の However 以降が筆者の主張。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: What is the role of the second sentence?
questions.answer: It gives examples of the advantages of cities.
questions.why: 文の役割を問う型。答えではなく機能を見る。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Which arrow best shows the structure of the passage?
questions.arrow_answer:
cities good points
↓
countryside good points
↓
personal choice
questions.answer: City advantages → Countryside advantages → Personal choice
questions.why: 本文の流れを矢印で表す問題。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.

questions.question: Which is NOT stated in the passage?
questions.arrow_answer:
本文にある
⇔
本文にない
questions.answer: Cities are always better than the countryside.
questions.why: 本文はどちらが絶対に良いとは言っていない。
questions.passage:
Some people think that cities are the best places to live, while others prefer the countryside.
Cities have convenient transportation, many schools, hospitals, and job opportunities.
On the other hand, the countryside offers fresh air, quiet surroundings, and close relationships with neighbors.
Each environment has its own advantages.
However, it is important to consider what kind of life suits you best.
